root_cause.text: The `cdf_read_property_info` function fails to validate whether the calculated property index is within the stream's bounds before reading the offset, leading to an out-of-bounds memory access.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces a `cdf_check_stream_offset` check for `tail * sizeof(uint32_t)` immediately before invoking `CDF_GETUINT32(p, tail)` to safely read the offset.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `cdf_read_property_info` function fails to validate whether the calculated property index is within the stream's bounds before reading the offset, leading to an out-of-bounds memory access.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces a `cdf_check_stream_offset` check for `tail * sizeof(uint32_t)` immediately before invoking `CDF_GETUINT32(p, tail)` to safely read the offset.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add bounds check before reading property offset to prevent out-of-bounds memory access.
intent_record.what: Added a bounds check before reading the property offset.
intent_record.how: Prevents out-of-bounds memory reads when accessing the stream offset.

grouping_result.intent: Add bounds check before reading property offset to prevent out-of-bounds memory access.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding the missing bounds check before reading the property offset, preventing the out-of-bounds memory access described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line reads the offset from memory using the property index without first validating if the calculated offset is within the bounds of the stream, leading to the out-of-bounds access.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement calculates and reads the property offset from the stream without verifying if the accessed index is within the stream's bounds, leading directly to the out-of-bounds memory access described in the root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Fix bug #61504, potential vuln. in fileinfo. update to 5.11
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 12cf930a403d0bbee0c40a5e93554cafd6b0895e
Author: Pierre Joye <pierre.php@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Mar 27 13:34:46 2012 +0200

    Fix bug #61504, potential vuln. in fileinfo. update to 5.11

diff --git a/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c b/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c
index a3f70220850..1f0f63b69d4 100644
--- a/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c
+++ b/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c
@@ -24,15 +24,18 @@
  * POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
  */
 /*
- * Parse composite document files, the format used in Microsoft O
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `cdf_read_property_info` calculates offsets via `CDF_GETUINT32(p, (i << 1) + 1)` within a loop bounded by `sh.sh_properties`. However, there is no validation to ensure that the memory accessed by `(i << 1) + 1` is within the stream's bounds before performing the read, leaving the out-of-bounds memory access vulnerability unresolved.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: MFH Update fileinfo to libmagic 5.00 and remove dependency on dirent.h on Windows
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 7a29c8f38677d1c7474c979836562488f6bbadec
Author: Scott MacVicar <scottmac@php.net>
Date:   Sun Mar 15 23:04:19 2009 +0000

    MFH Update fileinfo to libmagic 5.00 and remove dependency on dirent.h on Windows

diff --git a/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c b/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..9d8d042f163
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ext/fileinfo/libmagic/cdf.c
@@ -0,0 +1,1111 @@
+/*-
+ * Copyright (c) 2008 Christos Zoulas
+ * All rights reserved.
+ *
+ * Redistribution a
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `cdf_read_property_info` reads property offsets using `p[(i << 1) + 1]` inside the loop over `sh.sh_properties` without first checking if the index `(i << 1) + 1` falls within the boundaries of the stream buffer (`sst->sst_tab` plus its length). This missing validation matches the described root cause for the out-of-bounds memory access.
